steps.title: Simplify the first radical using the power rule
steps.content:
Apply the property $\sqrt[n]{a^m} = a^{m/n}$ to separate and simplify $\sqrt[5]{3^5 x^{45}}$.

$$\sqrt[5]{3^5 x^{45}} = \sqrt[5]{3^5} \cdot \sqrt[5]{x^{45}} = 3^{5/5} \cdot x^{45/5} = 3 \cdot x^9 = 3x^9$$

steps.title: Simplify the second radical
steps.content:
Apply the same property to $\sqrt[8]{2^8 x}$.

$$\sqrt[8]{2^8 x} = \sqrt[8]{2^8} \cdot \sqrt[8]{x} = 2^{8/8} \cdot x^{1/8} = 2 \cdot x^{1/8} = 2x^{1/8}$$

steps.title: Multiply all terms and combine like terms
steps.content:
Substitute the simplified radicals back into the original expression:

$$6 \cdot (3x^9) \cdot (2x^{1/8}) = 6 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 \cdot x^9 \cdot x^{1/8}$$

Combine constants: $6 \cdot 3 \cdot 2 = 36$
Combine powers of $x$ using $x^m \cdot x^n = x^{m+n}$: $x^9 \cdot x^{1/8} = x^{9+1/8}$

Convert to a common denominator: $9 = \frac{72}{8}$, so $9 + \frac{1}{8} = \frac{72}{8} + \frac{1}{8} = \frac{73}{8}$

$$\text{Result} = 36x^{73/8}$$
